# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Istio PeerAuthentication policy for our microservices namespace. Configure mTLS mode to allow both plaintext and encrypted traffic for backward compatibility. This is for our Kubernetes cluster during migration to service mesh.
# Category: istio_permissive_mtls

apiVersion: "security.istio.io/v1beta1"  # Use the Istio security API version that matches your environment or a more recent stable release (e.g., v1alpha2, etc.)    
kind: "PeerAuthenticationPolicy"          # Corresponds to Kiali's Peers tab in UI   
metadata:                                # Required fields for each policy  
  name:                                                    # Unique ID of the Policy      (You can use a GUID if you like)      
spec:                                     
  selector:                                   # Which namespace is this rule applied to?     Can be namespaces, services etc.    Included here as '*' for all traffic  
    matchLabels:                           	# Label selectors in Kubernetes (like app=foo or version=bar) that the selected objects must satisfy      For example {'app': ‘my-application’}     These labels will be added to mutual TLS peers. When client side is matched against any label, it accepts mTLS connection for secure communication and no peer authentication would take place because both sides are already authenticated with each other at the end of their handshake process
    namespace: 'your-namespace'                 # Define your kubernetes namespaces or select all if you want to apply policy on any installed application in cluster.  Can be list('ns1', 'ns2') for multiple     NAMESPACE, and get_namespaced() function can handle this
    matchExpressions:                         	# A set of label key-value pairs that must exist for an object (like app=my-app) to select it. Existing objects are then selected based on those labels   For example { 'kubernetes.io/name': ‘foo’ } can be used as a shortcut notation if you want all pods in ns named foo
    peerAuthentication: true                     # Required field for enabling mTLS     Peer Authentication ensures that the client and server agree on an identity before they start exchanging data over any network segment. It's often enabled to allow both plaintext (default) & encrypted traffic  For backward compatibility, set it as false unless you want peer-authenticated communications with all peers for each destination service
    mtls:                                      # Required field enabling mutual TLS     Enables the use of Mutual Transport Layer Security between a client and server. The default is true but can be overridden by setting to 'false' if you want only plaintext or encrypted communication   For backward compatibility, set it as false unless explicitly stated otherwise
